body: Dear valued customer, we have detected suspicious activity on your account. Your access has been temporarily suspended to protect your funds. You must verify your identity within 12 hours to restore access. Click here: http://nationwidebank-verify.net/login and enter your full SSN, account number, and PIN.

If this was not you, click the link below immediately to revert the change and secure your account: http://linkedin-account-support.com/recover?uid=83920. If you do not act within 1 hour, your account may be permanently compromised.

body: Hello! Read more at techcrunch.com. To unsubscribe, click here.

subject: Your GitHub Pull Request #1042 has been merged

You can view the merge commit at: https://github.com/acme-corp/backend/commit/a3f9c21. Thanks for your contribution!

subject: Flash Sale: 40% off all items this weekend only!
body: Don't miss our biggest sale of the year! For this weekend only, get 40% off storewide. Use code FLASH40 at checkout. Shop electronics, clothing, home goods, and more. Free shipping on orders over $50. Sale ends Sunday at midnight. Shop now at our website.

subject: Monthly Product Update — Notion
body: Hi there! We've been busy improving Notion for you. Here's what's new this month: New AI writing assistant now available in all plans, improved database filtering with compound conditions, new calendar view for projects, and performance improvements across mobile apps. Check out our blog for detailed release notes.

subject: 50% off your first Coursera course — this week only
body: Level up your skills with 50% off your first Coursera course this week. Explore thousands of courses from top universities including Stanford, Yale, and MIT. Learn Python, machine learning, business, and more. Use code LEARN50 at checkout. Offer valid until Sunday.

subject: Meeting Request: Q3 Planning Session — This Friday at 2pm
body:
Hi,

I'd like to schedule a Q3 planning session for this Friday at 2:00 PM EST. The agenda will cover: departmental budget review, hiring plan for Q3, product roadmap prioritization, and OKR alignment across teams.

The meeting will be held in Conference Room B and also available via Zoom. Please confirm your attendance or suggest an alternative time if Friday doesn't work for you.

Best,
Sarah Johnson
Director of Product Strategy

subject: URGENT: Production Server Down — Immediate Action Required
body:
CRITICAL ALERT: Production server prod-us-east-1 has been unresponsive for the past 15 minutes. Error rate is at 100%. All user requests are failing. The on-call engineer (Mike Chen) has been notified but requires team lead approval to initiate failover protocol.

Please acknowledge this alert and authorize the failover immediately to minimize downtime.

System: prod-us-east-1
Uptime lost: 15 minutes
Estimated impact: 50,000+ users affected
Incident ID: INC-20240315-001

subject: Contract Review Needed by EOD Friday
body:
Hi,

The vendor contract for our new AWS infrastructure agreement needs your review and sign-off by end of day this Friday. Legal has already cleared the terms. We need your approval to proceed with the $2.4M annual commitment.

I've attached the contract summary. Please review and send your approval or any required amendments.

This is time-sensitive — the vendor's offer expires Friday.

Regards,
Mark Rivera
Legal Department

subject: Code Review Request: Authentication Refactor PR #2187
body:
Hi,

I've opened PR #2187 for the authentication system refactor. This PR replaces our legacy session-based auth with JWT tokens and adds OAuth2 support for Google and GitHub SSO. I'd appreciate your review before we merge to main.

Key changes:
- New JWT middleware
- OAuth2 provider abstraction layer
- Updated test coverage to 94%
- Migration guide for existing sessions

Link: https://github.com/acmecorp/backend/pull/2187

Thanks,
Priya

subject: Team Lunch Next Thursday — Please RSVP
body:
Hi everyone,

We're organizing a team lunch next Thursday at 12:30 PM at The Garden Bistro (123 Main St). This is a great opportunity to connect with colleagues from different departments.

Please RSVP by Tuesday so we can make the reservation. Reply with 'attending' or 'not attending', and let us know of any dietary restrictions.

Looking forward to seeing everyone!

Best,
HR Team

I look forward to our conversation.

subject: Project Alpha: Status Update Required by Monday
body:
Hi team leads,

Please send your Project Alpha status updates by Monday morning. I need to compile them for the board presentation on Tuesday.

Include: progress against milestones, current blockers, resource needs, and revised timeline if applicable.

Be concise — 1 page max per team. This is critical for Tuesday's board meeting.

Thanks,
Robert Kim
CTO, Acme Corp

subject: Security Vulnerability Disclosure: CVE-2024-1234
body:
SECURITY BULLETIN

A critical security vulnerability (CVE-2024-1234) has been identified in the JWT library we use (jsonwebtoken v8.5.1). This vulnerability allows token forgery attacks on systems using the HS256 algorithm.

Affected systems: All production API endpoints using JWT authentication.
Severity: CRITICAL (CVSS 9.8)
Required action: Upgrade to jsonwebtoken v9.0.0 immediately.

This must be patched within 24 hours per our security policy. Please acknowledge receipt.

subject: Customer Complaint: Order #88421 Still Not Received After 3 Weeks
body:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am extremely frustrated. I placed order #88421 three weeks ago and it still has not arrived. Your website shows it was 'shipped' two weeks ago but the tracking number shows no movement for 10 days (USPS 9400111899223498765432).

I have tried calling customer service twice and waited on hold for 45+ minutes each time with no resolution. This is completely unacceptable.

I am demanding an immediate response with either a replacement shipment or a full refund. If I do not hear back within 48 hours, I will be filing a dispute with my credit card company and leaving reviews on all major platforms.

Sincerely,
Dissatisfied Customer

subject: Re: API Rate Limiting — Decision Needed Before Launch
body:
Hi,

Just circling back on the API rate limiting strategy we discussed in the architecture review. We're launching the public API next Monday and need a confirmed decision on the tiering model before we finalize the docs.

The two options:
1. Flat 1,000 req/min across all plans (simpler, but limits monetization)
2. Tiered: Free=100, Pro=1,000, Enterprise=unlimited (recommended by the team)

I need your call on this today — the DevRel team needs to update the docs and the engineering team needs to implement the rate-limit middleware. If we don't hear back, we'll default to Option 1.

Thanks,
Elena Vasquez
Head of Platform Engineering

body:
